_patch_meta.description: Pre-analysis patch for hope registry 78. Classifies all 44 None-status terms (14 extract, 30 delete); confirms 2 candidate_delete terms as delete; adds HF flag for G4253; adds schema investigation note for H6960B contamination and H7663B contamination.

operations.description: H6960B (qa.vah: to collect, id=305, occ=3, v=43) shares 43 verse records with sibling sub-gloss H6960A (qa.vah: to await, id=303, occ=48, v=44). The occ=3 vs v=43 discrepancy is severe — this is cross-sub-gloss verse sharing where the minority sub-gloss carries nearly all sibling verses. Analytically non-blocking: H6960A (44 verses) carries the full hope/await corpus. Analysis uses H6960A as the operative term. Claude Code to investigate: (1) whether H6960B verse records should be detached or whether the sub-gloss split is valid; (2) if valid, confirm which verses belong properly to the 'to collect' sense.

H7663B (sa.var: to await, id=304, occ=6, v=9) has v=9 vs occ=6 — 3 extra verse records, likely from Neh 2:13 and 2:15 where target_word is None (non-hope context). Contamination is minor. Analysis uses 6 legitimate hope verses; 3 spurious Nehemiah records noted.

Four None-status terms show occ < v (cross-sub-gloss verse sharing): H2342B (occ=3, v=10), H2342K (occ=8, v=10), H2427B (occ=1, v=6), H7951 (occ=5, v=6). All four are classified delete in this patch. Contamination is non-blocking. Claude Code to note for data audit.

set.status_note: ya.chal: to wait/wait sense (occ=18, 15 verses) — the waiting-with-expectation sense of ya.chal. CRL already recorded with patience (116). Core hope-patience bridge term. Psa 130:5 'I wait for the Lord'; Job 14:14 'all the days of my service I would wait'. 15 verses.

ya.chal: to wait/hope sense (occ=26, 26 verses) — the hope/trust sense of ya.chal. The forward-expectation dimension. Psa 31:24 'be strong, all you who wait for the Lord'; Isa 51:5 'the coastlands hope for me'. 26 verses.

be.tach: security/safely (adverb, occ=42, 41 verses) — the security/safety adverb from the ba.tach root (already extracted). Describes the condition of one who dwells safely/securely. Lev 25:18 'live in safety'; Psa 4:8 'dwell in safety'; Pro 3:29 'dwells trustingly'. 41 verses of the trust-security condition.
bit.chah: trust/confidence (occ=1, 1 verse — Isa 30:15 'in quietness and trust shall be your strength') — the trust-confidence noun. Single occurrence but programme-defining verse.

bat.tu.chot: security/safety (plural noun, occ=1, 1 verse — Job 12:6 'who bring their god in their hand, those whose god is their strength/security') — the security vocabulary plural form. Single occurrence; rounds out the ba.tach family.

kis.lah: confidence/folly (occ=2, 2 verses — Job 31:24 'if I have made gold my trust'; Psa 85:8 'let them not turn back to folly') — confidence vocabulary from the ke.sel/loin root. The two verses show both the legitimate (trust in God) and illegitimate (trust in gold) dimensions of confidence. 2 verses.

cha.sut: refuge/shelter (occ=1, 1 verse — Isa 30:2 'who take refuge in the shadow of Egypt') — refuge vocabulary from the cha.sah root (already extracted, 36v). Single occurrence as the refuge-shelter noun. Rounds out the refuge family.

miq.veh: collection/gathered waters (occ=3, 3 verses — Gen 1:10 'God called the dry land Earth, and the waters that were gathered together he called Seas'; Exo 7:19; Lev 11:36) — the collection/gathering sense of the miq.veh root. Related to H4723B (miq.veh: hope, already extracted). The root meaning of 'gathering together' underlies both hope (the gathering of expectation toward a point) and water collection. 3 verses — analytically significant for the semantic root analysis.

tiq.vah: cord/thread (occ=2, 2 verses — Jos 2:18, 21: Rahab's scarlet cord in the window) — the cord sense of tiq.vah. Analytically significant: the same word that means 'hope' (H8615B, 31v) also means a physical cord. The structural link is the root qa.vah (to bind/stretch/wait) — hope is the cord that holds; the Rahab cord is the physical expression of the same root. 2 verses.

na.vat: to look/regard (occ=72, 67 verses) — to look toward, fix one's gaze on. The visual dimension of hope: hope is directional looking. Psa 34:5 'those who look to him are radiant'; Isa 51:6 'lift up your eyes to the heavens'; Num 21:9 'when anyone looked at the bronze serpent'. 67 verses. The visual metaphor grounds the entire hope vocabulary in the act of directed attention.

sa.var: to inspect/examine (occ=3, 2 verses — Neh 2:13, 15: Nehemiah inspecting the walls of Jerusalem at night) — the inspection sense of sa.var. Related to H7663B (sa.var: to await, already extracted). The inspection sense illuminates the root: sa.var involves careful looking/examining — the same attentiveness that characterises hoping. 2 verses.

operations.description: epelpizō: to raise hope (occ=4, NO_VERSES) — candidate_delete confirmed. No verse records; thin data; content covered by G1679 (elpizō: to hope, 33v) and G1680 (elpis: hope, 50v). Delete confirmed.
qa.veh: line (occ=9, NO_VERSES) — candidate_delete confirmed. The line/measuring-line sense of qa.vah; no verse records; covered by H6957B (line, 13v) and H8615A (tiq.vah cord, 2v). Delete confirmed.

set.exclusion_reason: chul: tremble/fear sense (occ=12) — trembling/writhing before danger. The pain-writhing dimension, not hope. Content covered by H2342I (writhe in pain) and H2342K (anticipate anxiously). Delete.
chil: be firm (occ=3, contaminated v=10 — cross-sub-gloss bleed, OP-003) — be firm/strong sense of chul. Not hope vocabulary. Contamination noted. Delete.
chul: give birth (occ=9) — birthing/travail sense. Pain/labour not hope. Delete.
chul: writhe in pain/wound (occ=25) — pain and wounding. Not hope vocabulary. Cross-registry with distress/grief. Delete.
chul: dance/shake (occ=10) — dancing/shaking sense of chul. Not hope vocabulary. Delete.

chul: anticipate/wait anxiously (occ=8, contaminated v=10, OP-003) — anxious anticipation/writhing with pain. While tangentially related to tense waiting, the semantic weight is anxiety and pain rather than hope. CRL with anguish (005) already noted. Contamination noted. Delete.
chol: sand (occ=23) — sand as a material. No hope content. Root bleed from chul family.
chel: rampart/fortress (occ=9) — military fortification. No hope content. Root bleed from chul.
chil: agony/anguish (occ=6) — pain and anguish. Cross-registry with anguish (005) as CRL. Not hope vocabulary in primary sense. Delete.
chi.lah: agony (occ=1, contaminated v=6, OP-003) — agony sub-gloss. Contaminated. Delete.
che.lah: bulwark/rampart (occ=1) — military fortification. No hope content. Root bleed.
chal.cha.lah: anguish/trembling (occ=4) — anguish and terror. Not hope vocabulary. Cross-registry with anguish.

ke.sil: fool (occ=70) — foolishness. Root bleed from ke.sel/loin/hope family (H3689 already extracted). The fool sense is the negative: the foolish person misplaces confidence. Analytically interesting but not independently part of the hope vocabulary. 70 verses would skew coverage. Delete.
ke.sil: constellation (Orion, occ=1) — astronomical. Root bleed from ke.sel. No hope content.
ke.si.lut: stupidity (occ=1) — foolishness noun. Root bleed. No hope content.
ka.sal: be stupid (occ=1) — foolishness verb. Root bleed. No hope content.
ma.chol: dance (occ=6) — dancing. Root bleed from chul. No hope content.
me.cho.lah: dance (occ=8) — dancing. Root bleed from chul. No hope content.
qo.veh: Kue (place name, occ=4) — geographic location (Egypt/Asia Minor region). Root bleed from miq.veh. No hope content.
ne.vekh: spring (occ=1) — spring/water source. No hope content. Bleed.
sa.khal: be foolish (occ=8) — foolishness. Root bleed from ke.sel family. No hope content.
qav: cord/measuring-line (occ=12, v=2, below 20% — OP-003 note) — measuring-line sense. Content covered by H8615A (tiq.vah cord, extracted) and H6957B. Low coverage. Delete.

qav: cord/line (occ=13, 13 verses) — measuring-line in architectural/prophetic contexts. The cord sense is covered by H8615A (tiq.vah cord, Rahab narrative). The measuring-line sense (Eze 40 temple measurements, Isa 28:17 plumb line) does not carry hope semantic content independently. Delete.
qav.qav: might (occ=4, v=2) — uncertain meaning, possibly 'might' or a place name. Thin data; no hope content. Delete.

sha.lah: to prosper/be at ease (occ=5, contaminated v=6, OP-003) — ease and prosperity. This term belongs to the peace registry (117, where it is extracted). It entered the hope registry as bleed from the ease/security proximity. Contamination noted. Delete.
sha.lah: to neglect (occ=2) — negligence sense. No hope content.
she.leh: be safe (Aramaic, occ=1) — Aramaic ease/safety. Belongs to peace registry. Delete.
sha.lu: prosperity (occ=1) — ease/prosperity. Belongs to peace registry (117, extracted there). Bleed. Delete.
sha.lev: at ease (occ=8) — ease. Belongs to peace registry (117, active there). Bleed. Delete.
shal.vah: ease/prosperity (occ=8) — belongs to peace registry (117, extracted there). Bleed. Delete.
she.li: quietness (occ=1) — belongs to peace registry (117, extracted there). Bleed. Delete.
ofeilō: to owe/be obligated (occ=50) — debt and obligation vocabulary. No hope semantic content. Bleed from pro- prefix proximity to proelpizō (G4276, already extracted). Delete.

pro: before/in front of (preposition, occ=245, v=48, 20% — OP-003 note) — Greek preposition 'before' (temporal/spatial). Entered registry as prefix component of proelpizō (G4276: to hope beforehand, already extracted). The bare preposition carries no hope content. Delete.

operations.description: pro (before, occ=245) — 48 verses in export, 20% coverage. Classified delete in OP-049; flag added for record completeness.

_patch_summary.note: After application, projected active inventory: 21 existing extracted + 11 newly extracted = 32 active terms. Total verses estimated ~550-570 (adding ya.chal sub-glosses ~41v, be.tach security ~41v, na.vat ~67v, and other smaller terms). Re-export required before Session B analysis. The hope registry has a notably well-curated existing extraction — the 44 None-status terms contain significant bleed from the peace/ease vocabulary and from the chul/pain family, neither of which is hope proper.
